Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 — Search emails for all Heartland-related communications Hana sent or received
Use search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042"). Expected discoveries: (a) Marcus's request to verify invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 (email 152e45f552334da9a5a804457ac646f6); (b) Hana's incorrect confirmation that the invoice amount is correct (email 907b00bb34004d0987b67972b4233825); (c) Hana's email to Elena citing 8 moves at $1,200 each with 4 completed, errors because the correct rate is $1,600 and 5 moves were completed (email fca011bad4d3485e9d2d1de98e6f79b8); (d) Marcus's rate increase request specifying July 1, 2026 (email 0f4e6b055eb2440096638ce048282761); (e) Hana's reply incorrectly stating "beginning of Q4 2026" instead of July 1 (email fbc1ccce28d84555ba5f7c115d9d2760); (f) Hana's YTD email to Elena stating $90,000 when the correct figure is $92,150 (email 36ad47d6dec34b2d855819d9f106c794).
OE 2 — Search Slack for Heartland discussions including bill values policy and YTD thread
Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "bill values" or "YTD" or "$90,000"). Expected discoveries: (a) policy reminder message (TS: 1775169216.794573) in channel C005 stating all bill values must reflect corrected amounts based on verification, not vendor-requested amounts, establishing that the $12,800 bill should have been updated to $8,000; (b) the YTD thread including the request (TS: 1775168633.508013), Hana's clarification (TS: 1775168840.336863), the reply to exclude the disputed Q1 invoice (TS: 1775168855.168218), Hana's incorrect $90,000 figure (TS: 1775168881.939807), and the follow-up asking Hana to give Elena visibility (TS: 1775168916.185643).
OE 3 — Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills
Use quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: "Heartland"). Find all bills associated with Heartland Movers including the disputed Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800. Verify whether the bill has been updated to reflect the corrected amount of $8,000 (5 accepted moves × $1,600) or still shows the original vendor-requested amount.
OE 4 — Search Airtable for Heartland relocation records to verify accepted and disputed moves
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: tblRelocations01, field_name: "Company" or "Special Requirements", value: "Heartland" or individual employee names). Verify the five accepted moves (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Ines Kovac, Marcus Webb, Tanya Reeves) and confirm the per-move rate of $1,600, establishing the correct total of $8,000. Also confirm that the three disputed line items (Lily Marchetti: cancelled, Danielle Osei : cancelled, Priya Venkatesh: handled by Swift Relocations) are not legitimate Heartland charges.
OE 5 — Look up Elena Rostova's and Marcus Thorne's contact details
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Get elena.rostova@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com for correction emails.
OE 6 — Send corrected email to Elena about the invoice dispute
Use send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Subject referencing Heartland invoice correction. Content must: correct the per-move rate from $1,200 to $1,600, correct the number of completed moves from 4 to 5, correct the accepted total from $9,600 to $8,000, and confirm the disputed amount is $4,800 (3 moves × $1,600).
OE 7 — Send corrected email to Marcus about invoice confirmation being wrong
Use send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject referencing retraction of Heartland invoice confirmation. Content must: retract Hana's earlier confirmation that invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 was accurate, explain that the bill has not been updated from the vendor-requested $12,800 to the corrected $8,000 (5 accepted moves × $1,600), and note that the original confirmation was in error per the policy that bill values must reflect corrected verified amounts.
OE 8 — Send corrected email to Marcus about rate increase timing
Use send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Subject referencing Heartland rate increase correction. Content must: correct the implementation date from "beginning of Q4 2026" to July 1, 2026, and confirm the analysis will be based on the correct effective date.
OE 9 — Send corrected email to Elena about YTD total
Use send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Subject referencing Heartland YTD correction. Content must: correct the year-to-date total from $90,000 to $92,150 excluding the disputed Q1 invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042.
OE 10 — Send corrected Slack message in the Heartland thread
Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, thread_ts: 1775168633.508013). Post a reply in the same Slack thread where Hana originally stated $90,000 (TS: 1775168881.939807). Payload must: correct the YTD figure from $90,000 to $92,150 so the full thread reflects the accurate number.
OE 11 — Search QuickBooks for Terraform Digital estimate EST-2026-0423
Use quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "EST-2026-0423" or "Terraform Digital" or "preliminary estimate"). Find the estimate at $6,560 and check its current status, whether it has been finalized, sent, or is still incomplete.
OE 12 — Search QuickBooks for duplicate stipend records
Use quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: "STIP-2026-0089" or "STIP-2026-0094" or "STIP-2026-0097" or "stipend duplicate"). Find the three flagged duplicate stipend records and confirm their current status, STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, $89, paid), STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89, admin hold), STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45, admin hold) and whether they are creating any downstream issues in the books.
OE 13 — Search QuickBooks for Mosaic damage claim credit memo and accrual entry
Use quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "CM-2026-0415" or "Mosaic credit memo") and quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: "Mosaic accrual" or "ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC"). Find the credit memo (CM-2026-0415, -$90,000) and the accrual entry (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, $90,000 contingent liability). Verify the figures match the agreed claim amounts, $90K prototype value, $50K insurance coverage, $40K MoveOps direct exposure. Cross-reference against email communications about the Mosaic damage incident to confirm what was agreed.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

While the email incidentally mentions '$8,000 for 5 legitimate moves' as context in the YTD correction, it does not frame this as a correction of previously stated wrong per-move rate/count figures.
